risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: The project operates in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan, a region with substantial political and governance fragility. The Systematic Operations Risk-Rating Tool maintains a 'Substantial' rating for Political and Governance risk across all review periods, indicating persistent governance challenges that constrain implementation.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance [Risk Category Rating]: Substantial (at Approval 20 Jun 2019), Substantial (Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 09 - 10 Jan 2025), Substantial (Proposed Rating)

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints Linked to Governance Fragility
risk_summary: The project faces substantial institutional capacity challenges for implementation and sustainability, rated as 'Substantial' risk. This is compounded by governance fragility in the region, affecting the ability of implementing agencies to execute project activities effectively, as evidenced by slow HEIS implementation pace.
evidence_quote: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability [Risk Category Rating]: Substantial (at Approval 20 Jun 2019), Substantial (Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 09 - 10 Jan 2025), Substantial (Proposed Rating)

risk_title: Slow Implementation Pace Due to Administrative/Governance Delays
risk_summary: The Modern Irrigation Technologies component is experiencing significant implementation delays. HEIS (High Efficiency Irrigation Systems) implementation remains at only 9% achievement against a 45% target, with delays attributed to PC-1 revision approval processes, indicating governance and administrative bottlenecks affecting project delivery.
evidence_quote: Achievement against this indicator remains at 9 percent, as the pace of implementation of HEIS is slow due PC-1 revision and required concerted efforts.

risk_title: Farmer Withdrawal from HEIS Due to Economic Pressures in Fragile Context
risk_summary: Many farmers have withdrawn from the HEIS scheme due to high installation costs, suggesting economic vulnerability and potential social tensions in the project area. This withdrawal pattern may reflect broader livelihood pressures in the fragile Khyber Pakhtunkhwa region affecting project beneficiary participation.
evidence_quote: While efforts were made to increase the amount of land under HEIS, many farmers withdrew from the scheme due to high cost of installation.
